input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:

seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

input.initialPrompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText: The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate it to the modern `.runsettings` format after upgrading MSTest packages. Let me look at their files to understand the current configuration.

data.content: The `.testsettings` format is **deprecated** in modern MSTest (v3+). You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's the equivalent configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Equivalent to TestTimeout property (30 seconds) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test timeout in milliseconds -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Replaces DeploymentEnabled from .testsettings -->
    <DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled>
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
Here's the migration summary:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `<Property name="TestTimeout" value="30000"/>` | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout></MSTest>` |
| Assembly resolution / deployment | `<MSTest><DeploymentEnabled>false</DeploymentEnabled></MSTest>` |
| CodeCoverage DataCollector (uri v1.0) | `<DataCollectors>` section with updated uri v2.0 |

**To use it**, either:
1. Run: `dotnet test --settings local.runsettings`
2. Or add to your `.csproj`:
   ```xml
   <PropertyGroup>
     <RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>
   </PropertyGroup>
   ```

The old `local.testsettings` file can be deleted once you've verified the new settings work. The key difference is that MSTest v3 no longer reads `.testsettings` files — only `.runsettings` is supported.
